name: Fair Lending
description: Campaigns for credit products, loans, mortgages, and financial services subject to anti-discrimination lending laws. Triggers restrictions on demographic targeting to prevent discriminatory lending practices.

regulatory_frameworks.name: US ECOA
regulatory_frameworks.summary: Equal Credit Opportunity Act. Prohibits credit discrimination based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex, marital status, age, or receipt of public assistance.

regulatory_frameworks.name: US Fair Housing Act (lending)
regulatory_frameworks.summary: FHA prohibits discriminatory mortgage lending practices, including advertising that indicates preference based on protected characteristics.

guidance: Fair lending rules overlap significantly with fair housing when the credit product is a mortgage. Both FHA and ECOA apply. The key governance concern is audience targeting that excludes or skews away from protected classes — even via proxy signals like zip code or language.
